wearables.body: Wearables gets its own page because the surrounding discussion around Humane keeps needing this sharper distinction. Used well, it turns a vague AI-adjacent gesture into a claim someone can test, budget for, or push back on.

askNext: What can actually be done with the weights in this setting: inspect, adapt, host, or merely admire?
Which parts of the system remain opaque even if the weights are accessible?
Is the strategic value here the model's behaviour, the rights around it, or the ability to run it yourself?
weights.body: People talk as though access to weights resolves philosophy. It usually just moves the argument to licensing, cost, and who now has to operate the thing.
weights.example: "They said the weights were available as though that settled the harder questions about trust, licensing, and whether anyone should deploy them unescorted."
weights.misuse: By treating weights as synonymous with understanding, openness, or a complete product. Having access to weights matters, but it does not by itself explain data provenance, system quality, tooling, safety controls, or whether the thing belongs anywhere near production.
weights.practicalMeaning: In practice it usually means portability, distribution, storage, deployment constraints, adaptation, and endless arguments over whether access to weights counts as openness or merely a different pricing model.

translations.label: operator meaning
translations.text: The part of the model you can move, adapt, and argue about owning.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: The learned numeric state that gives a built model its behaviour.

weights.whyExists: Because a model is not merely an idea on paper. It becomes operational through the learned values that encode its statistical behaviour.

askNext: What exact meaning of Windows is being used here?
What changes operationally if this label is removed from the sentence?
Is this naming a mechanism, a vendor choice, a risk, or a wish?
windows.body: Windows earns its own page because branded nouns often hide decisions about platform control, pricing, permissions, and operational dependency. This entry keeps the name visible without letting it do the architecture's work.
windows.example: "Windows came up in the review, which was useful only after the team clarified whether they meant the product, the platform, the model family, or the vendor relationship."
windows.misuse: It gets abused when the name is used as a substitute for specifying the exact product surface, model, API, entitlement, pricing constraint, or operational dependency under discussion.
windows.practicalMeaning: In practice it usually means someone should ask what is being bought, integrated, routed through, depended on, or compared before the logo is allowed to stand in for the architecture.

translations.label: procurement meaning
translations.text: Treat the name as a dependency to inspect, not a badge to admire.

windows.warningLabel: A brand-shaped noun is not a technical specification.
windows.whyExists: The term matters because discussions around Microsoft Copilot often need to separate the actual technical choice from the brand, distribution channel, contract, or ecosystem wrapped around it.

askNext: What guardrails exist when the editor can modify multiple files quickly?
Which gains come from better context inside the IDE, and which come from lowering the user's standards?
Who reviews the fast changes with enough context to reject them?
windsurf.body: The sales pitch is flow. The operational question is whether the flow ends before or after the regression.
windsurf.example: "Windsurf kept the team in flow until the generated refactor reached production and introduced a more expensive kind of interruption."
windsurf.misuse: It is marketed as if constant AI presence dissolves the need for design judgement, review discipline, or the occasional suspicion that the machine may be freelancing. The editor can speed up real work. It can also accelerate a bad idea with unusual grace.
windsurf.practicalMeaning: In practice Windsurf usually means more generated diffs, faster navigation through unfamiliar code, less tab switching, and a sharper need for tests because the tool can now create confident mistakes at native speed.

translations.label: developer meaning
translations.text: A more deeply embedded coding assistant that can help right where the work is happening.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: An AI-heavy editor that reduces context switching while increasing the importance of sober review.

windsurf.whyExists: It exists because developers want help where the work already happens, not in a separate tab that requires manual copy-pasting and a fresh performance of context every ten minutes.

workflow-software.body: Workflow software belongs in the dictionary because implementation detail changes the product conversation. The useful question is not whether the term sounds modern, but what constraint, cost, interface, or failure mode it names.
workflow-software.example: "The team said workflow software as though it closed the matter; the postmortem revealed it was where the matter had been hiding."
workflow-software.misuse: It gets abused when the label is treated as a solution rather than a place where engineering still has to make choices, measure trade-offs, and own the consequences.

askNext: What is isolated by the worktree, and what shared state still leaks across tasks?
How will changes be reviewed, merged, kept, or discarded?
Do untracked files, environment secrets, and generated artefacts need to be copied or excluded?
worktree.body: Worktrees are boring in the way useful tools often are. They do not make agents safe. They make their edits easier to contain, inspect, and throw away.
worktree.example: "The worktree let three agents work in parallel, which was helpful until everyone remembered they still shared one confused package cache."
worktree.misuse: It gets treated as a sandbox. It is not one. A worktree isolates files and branches, not credentials, networks, databases, or the agent's ability to run a regrettable command.
worktree.practicalMeaning: In AI-assisted development, it usually means giving a coding agent or subagent a separate branch and directory so it can edit, test, and report back without colliding with the main checkout or another agent's work.

translations.label: developer meaning
translations.text: Another working directory attached to the same repository, useful for parallel branches.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: A practical way to let agents work beside you without immediately trampling the main checkout.

worktree.warningLabel: A worktree is branch isolation, not a security boundary.
worktree.whyExists: It exists because parallel work is useful and mixing every branch, experiment, dependency state, and agent task into one checkout is how small mistakes become local archaeology.

askNext: What specific predictive behaviour suggests a useful internal model rather than clever pattern matching?
How much of the claim comes from experiments, and how much from the romance of the phrase?
Does the system actually plan better, or do we simply enjoy the story more?
world-model.body: Some phrases arrive in public life already overpromoted. This one barely waits to leave the paper.
world-model.example: "Someone said world model and the meeting immediately upgraded from product review to metaphysical salon."
world-model.misuse: By treating any evidence of internal structure as proof of deep understanding, common sense, or machine comprehension of life itself. The phrase is often used at exactly the point where caution should become more fashionable.
world-model.practicalMeaning: In practice it usually means a model has learned enough regularity to predict useful consequences, not that it has joined the human condition. It is a technical claim that acquires mystical overtones the moment it leaves the lab.

translations.label: research meaning
translations.text: A claim that the system has learned structured regularities useful for prediction and planning.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: A technical idea that often acquires grander implications once it reaches the public.

world-model.whyExists: The term exists because systems that can anticipate structure, consequence, and continuity often perform better than systems that merely react to the next local cue.
